1st International Conference on Recent Advances in Computer Engineering  |  ICRACE 2026  |  23–24 April 2026  |  MES Wadia College of Engineering, Pune 
ICRACE 
Introduction 
3 •Interviews are a key gateway for engineering students, requiring both technical

knowledge and communication skills. 
•Academic training focuses on problem-solving but often ignores delivery aspects like 
confidence, fluency, and pacing. 
•LLM-based tools enable automated interview preparation and improve accessibility. 
•However, real performance depends on both correctness and communication quality. 
•To address this, we propose a Hybrid AI Interview Coach integrating personalization, 
adaptive progression, and real-time speech analysis.

1st International Conference on Recent Advances in Computer Engineering  |  ICRACE 2026  |  23–24 April 2026  |  MES Wadia College of Engineering, Pune 
ICRACE 
 Problem Statement 
4 
 1st International Conference on Recent Advances in Computer Engineering  |  ICRACE 2026  |  23–24 April 2026  |  MES Wadia College of Engineering, Pune •Existing interview preparation tools focus primarily on technical correctness, neglecting 
delivery aspects such as confidence, tone, and fluency.

•Students lack access to real-time, objective feedback on how they communicate during 
interviews. 
•Static question banks provide repetitive, non-adaptive practice and fail to simulate real 
interview dynamics. 
•Human coaching provides rich feedback but is expensive and not scalable, while 
chatbot-based systems ignore paralinguistic features of speech. 
•There is no unified system that simultaneously evaluates what a candidate says (semantics),

how they say it (speech delivery), and adapt to the user difficulty . 
ICRACE 
5 
Literature Review 
•LLM-based systems: Enable dynamic Q&A but suffer from hallucination and lack of structured 
progression 
•RAG-based methods: Improve personalization using resumes but focus only on text semantics 
•Speech analysis systems: Evaluate confidence and fluency, but are used independently of 
interview logic 
Observed research gap

interview logic 
Observed research gap 
•Existing systems fail to integrate semantic understanding (what is said) with speech delivery analysis 
(how it is said) in a unified framework. 
•Current tools lack adaptive, resume-aware progression, resulting in non-personalized and static
